scenarios.description: String overload: non-empty fragment with non-null context returns expected nodes list
scenarios.precondition: Use a stub TreeBuilder whose parseFragment(Reader,Element,String,Parser) returns a List containing two distinct test Nodes; parser constructed with this stub
scenarios.expectedResult: returned List size is 2 and contains exactly the two stub Nodes in order
pseudocode: GIVEN a Parser with stub TreeBuilder returning two nodes; String fragment=""; Element ctx=new Element("div"); baseUri="http://test"
WHEN calling parser.parseFragmentInput(fragment, ctx, baseUri)
THEN assert returned list size == 2 and elements match stub nodes

scenarios.description: String overload: non-empty fragment with null context returns expected nodes list
scenarios.precondition: Use stub TreeBuilder returning one test Node; context parameter is null
scenarios.expectedResult: returned List size is 1 and contains the stub Node
pseudocode: GIVEN a Parser with stub TreeBuilder returning one node; String fragment="<p/>"; Element ctx=null; baseUri="uri"
WHEN calling parser.parseFragmentInput(fragment, ctx, baseUri)
THEN assert returned list size == 1 and element matches stub node

scenarios.description: String overload: empty fragment returns empty list
scenarios.precondition: Use stub TreeBuilder returning empty list for empty input
pseudocode: GIVEN a Parser with stub TreeBuilder returning empty list; String fragment=""; Element ctx=new Element("span"); baseUri="u"
WHEN calling parser.parseFragmentInput(fragment, ctx, baseUri)
THEN assert returned list is empty

scenarios.description: String overload: null baseUri passed to parseFragment
scenarios.precondition: Use stub TreeBuilder that records baseUri input; baseUri is null
scenarios.expectedResult: returned List matches stub output and stub recorded baseUri==null
pseudocode: GIVEN a Parser with stub TreeBuilder recording baseUri; String fragment="<x/>"; Element ctx=new Element("x"); baseUri=null
WHEN calling parser.parseFragmentInput(fragment, ctx, baseUri)
THEN assert stub received baseUri null and returned list matches stub

scenarios.description: String overload: underlying Reader throws IOException leading to UncheckedIOException
scenarios.precondition: Use a custom Reader stub whose read(...) throws IOException on first call; no TreeBuilder stub needed
scenarios.expectedResult: java.io.UncheckedIOException is thrown; no nodes returned
pseudocode: GIVEN a Parser with real HtmlTreeBuilder; override Reader to throw IOException; String fragment="x"; ctx=new Element("x"); baseUri="u"
WHEN calling parser.parseFragmentInput(fragment, ctx, baseUri)
THEN assert throws UncheckedIOException

scenarios.description: Reader overload: non-empty fragment with non-null context returns expected nodes list
scenarios.precondition: Use Parser with stub TreeBuilder returning a list of three stub Nodes; Reader is StringReader on "AAA"
scenarios.expectedResult: returned List size == 3 with stub Nodes in order
pseudocode: GIVEN a Parser with stub TreeBuilder returning three nodes; Reader reader=new StringReader("AAA"); ctx=new Element("e"); baseUri="b"
WHEN calling parser.parseFragmentInput(reader, ctx, baseUri)
THEN assert returned list size == 3 and contents match stub nodes

scenarios.description: Reader overload: non-empty fragment with null context returns expected nodes list
scenarios.precondition: Use Parser with stub TreeBuilder returning zero nodes; context null
pseudocode:
GIVEN a Parser with stub TreeBuilder returning empty list; Reader reader=new StringReader("

"); ctx=null; baseUri="u"
WHEN calling parser.parseFragmentInput(reader, ctx, baseUri)
THEN assert returned list is empty

scenarios.description: Reader overload: empty fragment returns empty list
scenarios.precondition: Use stub TreeBuilder returning empty list for any input; Reader=new StringReader("")
pseudocode: GIVEN a Parser with stub TreeBuilder returning empty list; Reader=new StringReader(""); ctx=new Element("e"); baseUri="b"
WHEN calling parser.parseFragmentInput(reader, ctx, baseUri)
THEN assert returned list is empty

scenarios.description: Reader overload: null baseUri passed through to stub
scenarios.precondition: Use stub TreeBuilder that records baseUri; Reader=new StringReader("<x>"); ctx=new Element("x"); baseUri=null
scenarios.expectedResult: stub recorded baseUri==null and returned list matches stub
pseudocode: GIVEN a Parser with stub TreeBuilder; Reader=new StringReader("<x>"); ctx=new Element("x"); baseUri=null
WHEN calling parser.parseFragmentInput(reader, ctx, baseUri)
THEN assert stub received baseUri null and returned nodes

scenarios.description: Reader overload: underlying Reader throws IOException leading to UncheckedIOException
scenarios.precondition: Use a Reader stub whose read(...) throws IOException immediately; Parser with real TreeBuilder
scenarios.expectedResult: java.io.UncheckedIOException is thrown; no nodes returned
pseudocode: GIVEN a Parser with real XmlTreeBuilder; Reader stub throws IOException on read; ctx=new Element("e"); baseUri="b"
WHEN calling parser.parseFragmentInput(reader, ctx, baseUri)
THEN assert throws UncheckedIOException
